summary: The path-injection finding (CWE-22) persists across all attempts because the sanitize_file_path method uses os.path.commonpath without canonical path resolution (os.path.realpath), allowing bypass on case-insensitive filesystems or via symlinks. The http-response-splitting finding (CWE-113) was correctly mitigated with html.escape but remains flagged by the SAST tool as a false positive. The fixer loop failed to address the root cause of the path-injection vulnerability.
suggested_actions: Use os.path.realpath to resolve the base directory and the user-provided path to their canonical forms before comparing with os.path.commonpath.
Ensure the resolved path starts with the resolved base directory to prevent traversal via symlinks or case variations.
Consider marking the http-response-splitting finding as a false positive in the SAST tool configuration, as html.escape is sufficient to prevent CRLF injection.
Add explicit validation that file_name does not contain path separators or '..' before joining, as an additional defense layer.
